title: lupinus albus subsp. termis
intro: Lupinus albus subsp. termis, also known as the white lupin, is a perennial legume native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is rich in isoflavones, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of white lupin include its ability to support cardiovascular health, reduce oxidative stress, and exhibit potential anticancer properties. White lupin can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of menopause, digestive issues, and skin conditions.
